skill.name: clinical-study-info-extractor
skill.description: Batch extracts and verifies structured information (PMID, title, abstract, methodology, results, etc.) from clinical research literature using PMIDs. Use when the user wants to extract details from specific PMIDs.
skill.summary: Batch extract and verify structured clinical study data from PubMed using PMIDs.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 40 potential issues across 4 files. Manual evaluation confirms all are false positives. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections resulted from the scanner misidentifying markdown table formatting and JSON audit score fields as cryptographic patterns. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are documentation-only shell examples in markdown code blocks, not auto-executed commands. Network calls target the legitimate PubMed API (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) which is the skill's core function. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL to External API
low_findings.description: scripts/utils.py contains a hardcoded URL to the PubMed API endpoint. This is the skill's core intended function fetching clinical study data. The endpoint is a legitimate public NIH service. No credential exposure or data exfiltration risk.

content.user_title: Extract Clinical Study Data from PubMed IDs
content.value_statement: Researchers waste hours manually extracting data from clinical studies for systematic reviews. This skill automates batch extraction of structured information including title, abstract, methodology, and results from PubMed using standard PMID identifiers.
actual_capabilities: Batch fetch clinical study metadata from PubMed using PMID lists
Extract structured fields including title, abstract, DOI, journal, and authors
Parse and normalize PMID input from various separator formats
Format extracted data into markdown tables for easy review
Integrate with LLM extraction for detailed per-field analysis
limitations: Requires valid PubMed IDs (PMIDs) as input, cannot use DOIs or NCT numbers
Only extracts metadata and abstracts, not full-text article content
Output quality depends on completeness of available PubMed records
Does not support non-PubMed clinical databases or citation sources

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review
use_cases.description: Quickly extract structured data from hundreds of clinical studies by providing PMIDs for systematic review or meta-analysis preparation.

use_cases.title: Evidence-Based Medicine Research
use_cases.description: Gather and organize clinical evidence from PubMed studies into structured tables for academic papers or medical presentations.

use_cases.title: Clinical Trial Portfolio Management
use_cases.description: Extract and verify trial details from multiple studies to maintain a searchable database of clinical research findings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PMID Extraction
prompt_templates.scenario: New user learning the skill for the first time
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the clinical-study-info-extractor skill to fetch details for PMIDs 12345678, 23456789, and 34567890.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Extraction from Multiple PMIDs
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing a list of studies for a literature review
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract clinical study information from these PMIDs: 12345678, 23456789, 34567890, 45678901, 56789012. Format the output as a markdown table.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Extraction with Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensuring data quality with validation rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the clinical study info extractor on PMIDs 12345678, 23456789. Apply verification rules to check article type classification and sample size formatting per the reference guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Integrated Workflow with Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use with downstream analysis steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract study details from PMIDs 12345678, 23456789, 34567890. After extraction, identify which studies are randomized controlled trials and summarize their key findings.

output_examples.output: The skill returns a markdown table row with fields: PMID, article title, publication year, journal name, abstract text, DOI, article type classification, study population, sample size, intervention details, key findings, and author conclusions.

A markdown table with two rows. Each row contains the structured fields including translated Chinese content for most fields and original language for journal names. Verification rules are applied to confirm article type and sample size accuracy.

output_examples.input: Extract and verify PMID 45678901 with quality checks
output_examples.output: The extracted data is first produced as a table row, then a verification phase checks article type classification, numeric sample size validity, language compliance, and markdown format correctness. Any corrections are applied automatically.

best_practices: Validate PMIDs before extraction to avoid errors from invalid or outdated identifiers.
Review extracted data against original PubMed records to confirm accuracy of automated classification.
Use structured output formats like markdown tables for consistent downstream processing.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-PubMed identifiers such as DOIs or ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT) numbers.
Do not rely on extracted findings without verifying article type classification and sample size values.
Do not use this skill for full-text extraction; it only handles metadata and abstracts from PubMed.

faq.question: What is a PMID?
faq.answer: A PMID (PubMed Identifier) is a unique integer number assigned to each article in the PubMed database.

faq.question: How many PMIDs can I process at once?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but batch sizes of 10 to 50 are recommended for reliable processing.

faq.question: Does this skill extract full-text articles?
faq.answer: No, it extracts metadata and abstracts from PubMed. Full-text access requires separate publisher permissions.

faq.question: Can I use DOIs instead of PMIDs?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires PubMed IDs (PMIDs). DOIs are not supported as input identifiers.

faq.question: Is the extracted data verified for accuracy?
faq.answer: The skill applies automated verification rules for article type and sample size. You should confirm results against original sources.

faq.question: What languages does the output support?
faq.answer: The skill translates most fields to Chinese, except journal names which remain in the original language.
